name: Oliver Jackson
abstract: Oliver Jackson (April 28, 1933 – May 29, 1994), also known as Bops Junior, was an American jazz drummer.

works.t: Right This Way: I'll Be Seeing You

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: JPJ Quartet

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Allen
about_quotes.evidence: he took me on a bunch of tours around Europe which ended up accounting for probably about half of the work I do now, from tours that can be traced directly back to the contacts I made through Oliver Jackson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I did a record date I'm proud of called 'Teddy Wilson's All Starts'... we had Oliver Jackson on drums.

about_quotes.speaker: Budd Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: This is a group that was formed by Oliver Jackson, Bill Pimperton and myself after we left Earl in July of 1969.

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: You see, all those people in the group called Jazz of the Swing Era... We had Oliver Jackson, drums.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: There I am, and Doc Cheatham, I don’t remember this lady, but this is Max Roach and Oliver Jackson. So to me, just to be in the same room, and much less on a picture, is something that I’ll always cherish.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 16, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Pierre Boussaguet
about_quotes.evidence: That was a record under the name of Sweets, a quintet with Eddie “Lockjaw” Davis, Oliver Jackson drums, beautiful piano player Gerry Wiggins, and Major Holley on bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pierre Boussaguet interviewed by Matthieu Jaffre, Aix en Provence, France, July 28, 2020
